name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023

key_provisions.requirement: Personal data may be processed only for a lawful purpose for which the Data Principal has given consent, or for certain legitimate uses specified under the Act.

Consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous. Bundled consent (consent coupled with other terms as a condition of service) is not permitted. Consent request must be presented in clear plain language with a standalone option to withdraw.
Processing without consent is permissible only for: state functions, legal obligations, medical emergencies, employment purposes, or public interest — and only to the extent necessary.

Data fiduciary must: (a) ensure accuracy and completeness of personal data; (b) implement technical and organisational measures; (c) not retain data beyond the necessary period; (d) implement Data Retention Policy with defined timelines.

Data fiduciary cannot delegate processing responsibilities to sub-processors without explicit written consent from the Data Principal and binding contractual obligations on the sub-processor that are equivalent to those imposed on the fiduciary.

Processing of personal data of children (under 18) requires verifiable parental consent. Data fiduciary must not undertake tracking, behavioural monitoring, or targeted advertising directed at children.
Data Principal has the right to obtain a summary of personal data processed and the identities of all Data Processors and third parties with whom the data has been shared.
Data Principal has the right to correct inaccurate or misleading personal data, complete incomplete data, update outdated data, and erase data no longer necessary for the stated purpose.

Data Principal has the right to have grievances redressed. Data fiduciary must publish contact details of a Data Protection Officer or an authorised person, and respond to grievances within a prescribed period.

Entities designated as Significant Data Fiduciaries must appoint an Indian-resident Data Protection Officer, conduct periodic Data Protection Impact Assessments, and audit their algorithms for potential harm.
Transfer of personal data outside India is permitted only to countries notified by the Central Government. Contracts must ensure equivalent protection standards.
Data fiduciary must notify the Data Protection Board and affected Data Principals of any personal data breach in the prescribed form and within the prescribed timeline.

high_risk_clause_patterns: unlimited data sharing with third parties
